program_name: Parkinsons Speech Therapy - LSVT-Aligned Lesson Library

_provenance.based_on: LSVT LOUD daily home practice (Aurora Health Care patient handout x26031bc; LSVT Global Homework Helper structure).
_provenance._notes: Standard daily hierarchy: 6 sustained AHs, 6 high pitch glides, 6 low pitch glides, counting 1-10 in a loud voice, 10 functional phrases, then everyday sentences. Each step is exposed as a discrete drill the coach can grade individually.

categories.name: Vocal Warm-up — Sustained AH
categories.goal: Build vocal-fold strength and breath support with the canonical LSVT LOUD long-AH exercise.

lesson.goal: Hold AH as long as possible at LOUD, steady volume — the foundation of LSVT LOUD.
lesson.instructions_for_agent: Listen for: long duration, steady (no tremor or break), full voice (not breathy or whispered).
lesson.success_criteria: AH is sustained for several seconds at full voice without breathiness or pitch break.

main_task.instructions: Take a deep breath. Say AH in a loud voice and hold it as long as you can. Try to project your voice across the room.
main_task.focus: Loud, steady, full voice; no trail-off.

categories.name: Pitch Glides
categories.goal: Expand pitch range while keeping the loud voice.

lesson.goal: Glide AH from speaking pitch to as high as possible while staying LOUD.
lesson.instructions_for_agent: Reward an audible upward sweep that stays voiced and loud throughout.
what_to_emphasize: Keep the voice strong at the top
common_mistakes: Voice cuts out at the top
lesson.success_criteria: Continuous upward pitch glide on AH with the voice loud at start AND top.

main_task.instructions: Start with a loud AH, then glide your voice as high as you can. Hold the high note for five seconds.
main_task.focus: Smooth glide; loud at top; no cracking.

lesson.goal: Glide AH from speaking pitch to as low as possible while staying LOUD.
lesson.instructions_for_agent: Reward an audible downward sweep that stays voiced and loud at the bottom.
what_to_emphasize: Keep the voice strong at the bottom
common_mistakes: Voice cuts to a whisper at the bottom
lesson.success_criteria: Continuous downward pitch glide on AH with voice loud at start AND bottom.

main_task.instructions: Start with a loud AH, then glide your voice as low as you can. Hold the low note for five seconds.
main_task.focus: Smooth glide; loud at bottom; no whisper.

categories.name: Counting in a Loud Voice
categories.goal: Sustain LOUD voice across a sequence — bridge from sustained AH to connected speech.

lesson.goal: Count 1-10 in a strong, projected voice; pause for breath as needed without dropping volume.
lesson.instructions_for_agent: Listen for all ten digits in order, full voice on each, with breath pauses that don't drop loudness.
lesson.success_criteria: All ten numbers spoken loudly and clearly in order.

main_task.instructions: Count from 1 to 10 in a loud voice. Pause for a breath if you need to, but keep your voice strong all the way to ten.
main_task.focus: Loud on every number; no trailing off.

categories.name: Functional Phrases (LSVT LOUD)
categories.goal: Practise the canonical 10 LSVT LOUD functional phrases — the ones you actually say every day.

lesson.goal: Use a LOUD voice for the highest-frequency social phrases.
lesson.instructions_for_agent: Each phrase is short — listen for full voice through the entire phrase, including the final word.
what_to_emphasize: Project as if calling across a room
common_mistakes: Saying the phrase quietly because it's familiar
lesson.success_criteria: Phrase is fully audible and clear from first word to last.

main_task.instructions: Say each phrase in a loud, expressive voice. Project across the room. Finish the last word as strongly as the first.
main_task.focus: Loud start AND loud end; project; do not trail off.

lesson.goal: Project intent on action-oriented phrases that occur in real daily life.
lesson.instructions_for_agent: These are LSVT LOUD canonical functional phrases. Listen for full-voice projection on every word.
common_mistakes: Asking softly because asking feels polite
Voice fading on the second half
lesson.success_criteria: Each phrase is delivered with intent and full audibility throughout.

main_task.instructions: Say each phrase in a loud, expressive voice — like you really mean it.
main_task.focus: Project intent; loud through to the final word.

categories.name: Everyday Sentences
categories.goal: Carry the LOUD voice into longer breath groups with real-world content.

lesson.goal: Plan the breath, then deliver the whole sentence in a loud voice without breaking volume.
lesson.instructions_for_agent: Reward sentences finished at full volume. Penalise drop-off after the second-to-last word.
common_mistakes: Speaking too quickly to stay loud
lesson.success_criteria: Whole sentence is loud and intelligible from start to end.

content: I would love a cup of coffee.
It is good to see you.
main_task.instructions: Take a breath, then say the whole sentence in a loud voice — finish as strongly as you started.
main_task.focus: Loud through the period; finish strong.

lesson.goal: Carry intonation and full voice through to the question mark.
lesson.instructions_for_agent: Listen for an audibly rising end on questions and full volume on every word.
common_mistakes: Dropping volume on the last word
lesson.success_criteria: Question is clearly a question and is loud through the final word.

content: Would you like to go out to eat?
main_task.instructions: Ask each question in a loud voice. Let your pitch rise at the end.
main_task.focus: Pitch rise at end; full voice on the final word.
